content: First-episode psychosis: early intervention programs improve outcomes. Antipsychotic medication first-line—start low, go slow to minimize side effects; second-generation (aripiprazole, risperidone, olanzapine) often preferred initially due to lower EPS risk, though metabolic monitoring essential. Address comorbid substance use. Psychosocial interventions: CBT for psychosis, family psychoeducation, supported employment/education, case management. Duration of untreated psychosis correlates with poorer outcomes—rapid assessment/treatment critical. For mood disorders with psychotic features: antipsychotic + mood stabilizer/antidepressant. Medical/substance-induced: treat underlying cause. Clozapine for treatment-resistant cases. Long-term maintenance reduces relapse risk.
